name: Jalapeno Pepper Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove most of the seeds from the jalapeno peppers and chop coarsely.
You should have about 1/4 cup. In a blender or food processor, whirl all
of the peppers until finely ground. Place the ground vegetables and any
juice in a heavy-bottomed pan (8 to 10 quart). Stir in sugar and vinegar
until well blended.
2. Bring mixture to a full rolling boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
Stir in pectin all at once. Return to a full rolling boil; then boil,
stirring for one minute. Remove from heat and skim off any foam.
3. Ladle hot jelly into hot, sterilized half-pint jars, leaving 1/4 inch
headspace. Wipe rims clean and seal with hot lids and bands. Process in a
boiling water canner for five minutes.
